commands.name: azure-relay
commands.description: Connect directly to an existing Azure Relay instance.

options.name: azure-auth-token
options.description: An Azure authentication token (JWT) that can be used for any Azure management operations. If not specified, we'll use the Azure Identity SDK to try to authenticate you.

arguments.name: relay-connection-string
arguments.description: A connection string for an existing Azure Relay Hybrid Connection.

arguments.description: The base URL where traffic should be tunneled to.

commands.name: initialize
commands.description: Initializes your Azure Subscription with support for the ikspōz "auto" connection feature.

options.name: tenant-id
options.description: The ID of the Azure Tenant that should be authenticated against.

options.name: subscription-id
options.description: The ID of the Azure Subscription of the Azure Relay Instance that should be used.

options.name: resource-group
options.description: The Azure Resource Group name of the Azure Relay Instance that should be used.

options.name: relay-namespace
options.description: The Azure Relay namespace to create. If left unspecified, ikspōz will generate a unique namespace for you. (NOTE: This value must be globally unique to all of Azure.)

options.name: relay-namespace-location
options.description: The Azure locatation where you want the namespace to be created. For a full list of locations you can use: az account list-locations. Refer to https://azure.microsoft.com/en-us/global-infrastructure/geographies/#geographies for more information.
